perturbation.original_text:
Party B shall pay Party A the energy saving service fee at the stipulated time, otherwise:
2.1 If Party B fails to pay Party A the energy saving service fee by 15t h of the month and the delay is within 60 days, the daily penalty is 0.05% of the overdue payment.
2.2 If the delay is over 60 days, it is regarded that Party B has no ability to perform its payment obligation. Party A can enforce the Performance Guarantee by Party B to take all project assets. Party B shall pay the actual energy saving service fees at once and pay Party A losses.
perturbation.changed_text:
Party B shall pay Party A the energy saving service fee at the stipulated time, otherwise:
2.1 If Party B fails to pay Party A the energy saving service fee by 15t h of the month and the delay is within 60 days, the daily penalty is 0.05% of the overdue payment.
2.2 If the delay is over 60 days, Party A may, at its sole discretion, consider Party B to have no ability to perform its payment obligation. Party A can enforce the Performance Guarantee by Party B to take all project assets, or choose to renegotiate the payment terms. Party B shall pay the actual energy saving service fees at once and pay Party A losses.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty regarding Party A's actions if Party B delays payment. The original text stated that a delay of over 60 days *is regarded* as Party B's inability to pay, and Party A *can enforce* the performance guarantee. The modified version changes this to Party A *may, at its sole discretion, consider* Party B unable to pay and *can enforce* the performance guarantee *or choose to renegotiate*. This contradiction creates uncertainty about what action Party A will take, potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
Party B shall pay Party A the energy saving service fee at the stipulated time, otherwise:
2.1 If Party B fails to pay Party A the energy saving service fee by 15t h of the month and the delay is within 60 days, the daily penalty is 0.05% of the overdue payment.
2.2 If the delay is over 60 days, Party A may, at its sole discretion, consider Party B to have no ability to perform its payment obligation. Party A can enforce the Performance Guarantee by Party B to take all project assets, or choose to renegotiate the payment terms. Party B shall pay the actual energy saving service fees at once and pay Party A losses.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that after 60 days of delay, it *is regarded* that Party B cannot perform its payment obligation, giving Party A a defined course of action. The changed text introduces optionality for Party A ('may, at its sole discretion, consider') and adds an alternative action ('or choose to renegotiate the payment terms'). This directly contradicts the original text's implicit certainty, making it unclear whether Party A will enforce the guarantee, renegotiate, or do something else. This creates uncertainty and a direct contradiction, as the contract now offers conflicting paths of action, weakening the original enforcement obligation.

perturbation.original_text: During the contact period, Party A has the ownership of the CDQ and CDQ waste heat power generation systems. After the termination of the contact, Party B has the disposition right to the project.
perturbation.changed_text: During the contact period, Party A has the ownership of the CDQ and CDQ waste heat power generation systems. After the termination of the contact, Party B has the disposition right to the project, unless Party A and B agree otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that upon termination of the contract, Party B has the *disposition right* to the project. The modified version adds the phrase "unless Party A and B agree otherwise." This creates a contradiction because it introduces a condition where Party B might not have the disposition right, conflicting with the initial definitive statement. The term disposition right is being modified in a way that allows for an exception. The original section states Party B has the disposition rights, where as this changes and adds a clause that, Party A can agree otherwise. This leads to uncertainty regarding ownership after the termination of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the contact period, Party A has the ownership of the CDQ and CDQ waste heat power generation systems. After the termination of the contact, Party B has the disposition right to the project, unless Party A and B agree otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states that upon termination of the contract, Party B has the disposition right to the project. The modified version adds the phrase "unless Party A and B agree otherwise." This creates a contradiction because it introduces a condition where Party B might not have the disposition right, conflicting with the initial definitive statement. The term disposition right is being modified in a way that allows for an exception. The contradiction is strong enough that a reasonable reader would immediately recognize the sections as incompatible, specifically the change introduces uncertainty where there was none.

perturbation.original_text: The agreement can only be terminated after negotiation and agreement by Party A and B in writing. When the agreement is terminated, Party A has rights to dispose all assets of the recycling project.
perturbation.changed_text: The agreement can only be terminated after negotiation and agreement by Party A and B in writing, unless otherwise stated in this agreement. When the agreement is terminated, both Party A and B have rights to dispose all assets of the recycling project.
perturbation.explanation: In this change, I've introduced two contradictions. First, the original text dictates the agreement can *only* be terminated after negotiation and written agreement by both parties. The modified version adds "unless otherwise stated in this agreement," creating an exception that undermines the original condition. This opens the door for unilateral termination under unspecified circumstances. Second, the original states when the agreement is terminated, *Party A* has rights to dispose of all assets. But, the modified version gives *both Party A and B* the rights to dispose all assets. This intext contradiction creates a conflict about what party has the rights to dispose of the assets. Thus, creating a uncertainty in the text regarding disposal of the assets. Due to, the addition of 'unless otherwise stated in this agreement', allows either party to get out of the original agreement, leading to a lot of uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The agreement can only be terminated after negotiation and agreement by Party A and B in writing, unless otherwise stated in this agreement. When the agreement is terminated, both Party A and B have rights to dispose all assets of the recycling project.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a contradiction. Originally, termination required agreement by both parties. The addition of "unless otherwise stated in this agreement" allows for termination under unspecified circumstances, contradicting the initial requirement of mutual agreement. Furthermore, the original text stated Party A has rights to dispose of all assets, the modified version states that both party A and B have rights to dispose of all assets creating a conflict regarding which party has the rights to dispose of the assets. This directly contradicts the original agreement and creates uncertainty about asset disposal, thus the changed text contradicts the contradicted text.
